chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH BENCH AT SUKKUR Cr. Bail App. No. S – 1103 of 2025 Date of hearing Order with signature of Judge Fresh case 1. For orders on MA No.6928/2025 (U/A) 2. For orders on office objections at Flag-A 3. For orders on MA No.6583/2025 (Ex./A) 4. For hearing of pre-arrest bail 04.12.2025 Mr. Sheeraz Fazal, Advocate along with applicants..-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.- 1. Urgency is allowed. 2 to 4. Learned Counsel for the applicants contends that present pre- arrest bail application has been filed after the earlier pre-arrest bail application was not pressed on account of fresh ground being available. He further contends that no useful purpose will be served in case the present applicants are arrested first and then bail be acquired to them. Having heard the learned Counsel and gone through the record, learned Counsel has already availed three dates in order to show that once a pre-arrest bail application has been disposed of as not pressed, a subsequent attempt is to be made available to him. Today also, he has not shown any law in this regard. The present pre-arrest bail application has been filed after the pre-arrest bail application by the learned Sessions Court was dismissed by order dated 30.10.2025, whereas, the fresh ground, as is being called upon, is based upon an order dated 27.08.2025 whereby the co-accused has been granted post-arrest bail. Same apparently was available for the applicants before disposal of the earlier pre-arrest bail application however the applicants have preferred not to surrender them before the learned trial Court and seek bail. It seems that the applicants have been ill-advised for exercising the forum of pre-arrest bail application again and again without any reasonable explanation, same is not available.

Same apparently was available for the applicants before disposal of the earlier pre-arrest bail application however the applicants have preferred not to surrender them before the learned trial Court and seek bail. It seems that the applicants have been ill-advised for exercising the forum of pre-arrest bail application again and again without any reasonable explanation, same is not available. The present bail application without touching the merits, on account of maintainability, stands dismissed accordingly. J U D G E Abdul Basit
